title: Microchilus metallescens
taxonomy: Microchilus metallescens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Microchilus, which is a group of orchids that are primarily found in Central and South America. Microchilus metallescens is further classified as a member of the subfamily Apostasioideae. This species is known to be a rare and endangered orchid due to habitat loss and degradation. The classification of Microchilus metallescens is essential for understanding its evolutionary history and conservation status.

intro: Microchilus metallescens, also known as "Gulancha," is a type of climber vine in the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues.
